reasoning:
Verdict: REJECT (10% confidence)

Module Scores:
  Sentinel (CWE/OWASP Web Application Security): 50/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Watchdog (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Cisco AI Threat Taxonomy): 99/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Guardian (NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / UNICC Responsible AI): 5/100 [Critical risk] — 4 critical, 5 high, 5 medium

Arbitration Triggers:
  • REJECT trigger — score below 30: Guardian (5/100)
  • REJECT trigger — critical findings in Guardian: [guardian-1] Autonomous Content Authenticity Decisions Affecting Public Discourse Without Human Oversight; [guardian-2] High-Risk AI System With Zero Governance Documentation; [guardian-3] EU AI Act High-Risk Classification With No Conformity Controls; [guardian-4] No Bias Testing or Fairness Evaluation in Deepfake Detection and Fact-Checking Pipeline

Disagreement Resolution:
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) — Δ49 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Sentinel to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 5/100 (critical risk) — Δ45 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 5/100 (critical risk) — Δ94 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.

Confidence Factors:
  • Base: 1/3 module(s) independently support REJECT — 30% baseline conviction
  • -30% confidence: 3 disagreement(s) between modules
  • Average module score across 3 completed module(s): 51/100 (reported for context — not directly used in confidence calibration)

--- LLM-Enhanced Council Narrative ---
The strongest cross-module agreement is procedural rather than substantive: Sentinel [sentinel-1-70d3a5c4], Watchdog [watchdog-1], and Guardian [guardian-2] all indicate that the repository was effectively empty of the artifacts needed for verification. Sentinel and Watchdog therefore appropriately limit themselves to informational conclusions, stating that concrete web-security and LLM-specific findings cannot be validated. This means the technical security posture of the Flask/PostgreSQL/AWS application and any AI invocation paths remains largely unknown rather than demonstrated safe.

The main tension is between evidentiary standards. Guardian reaches a critical governance/compliance posture, asserting severe deficiencies such as lack of human oversight [guardian-1], high-risk EU AI Act nonconformity [guardian-3], and absent fairness/privacy/transparency controls [guardian-4], [guardian-6], [guardian-7], even though the same lack of repository evidence caused Sentinel and Watchdog to avoid concrete findings. That is not necessarily wrong—Guardian is reasoning from the application profile and the absence of governance artifacts—but it does create a conflict in confidence level: two modules say "cannot verify," while Guardian says "critical deficiency" across multiple domains.

Key gaps remain outside all three assessments. None of the modules meaningfully covered cloud/infrastructure security for the stated AWS deployment, software and model supply-chain integrity, or adversarial ML robustness for the non-LLM ResNet/BERT pipeline. As a result, the overall posture should be read as: governance risk appears plausibly high based on the stated use case and missing documentation, but technical application, infrastructure, and model-security risk cannot yet be reliably characterized without the actual codebase, deployment configuration, dependency manifests, and model lifecycle artifacts.

top_findings: No verifiable LLM integration source code available for VeriMedia AI
watchdog.summary: VeriMedia AI appears to use AI/ML components such as ResNet and BERT for media verification, but no source code and no explicit LLM stack were provided. As a result, no concrete LLM-specific security findings under the OWASP LLM Top 10 or Cisco attacker taxonomy can be validated.
watchdog.recommendation: 1) Provide source code for all AI invocation paths so prompt injection, output handling, tool use, and data exposure can be assessed. 2) Identify the exact AI stack in use, including any LLM APIs, orchestration frameworks, retrieval components, and autonomous moderation logic. 3) If LLM features exist, submit the files containing prompt templates, model calls, and downstream action handlers for targeted OWASP-LLM review.

top_findings: Insufficient source code provided for security analysis
sentinel.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a Flask-based AI media verification platform using PostgreSQL and deployed on AWS, but zero source files were provided for analysis. No concrete security findings can be produced without access to the actual codebase.
sentinel.recommendation: 1. Provide the complete source code repository so that static analysis can identify real vulnerabilities in authentication, input validation, secrets management, and API security. 2. Include dependency manifests (requirements.txt, Pipfile.lock) to enable known-CVE scanning. 3. Include deployment and infrastructure configuration files for transport security and cloud misconfiguration review.

top_findings: Autonomous Content Authenticity Decisions Affecting Public Discourse Without Human Oversight
High-Risk AI System With Zero Governance Documentation
EU AI Act High-Risk Classification With No Conformity Controls
guardian.summary: VeriMedia AI is an AI-powered media verification and content moderation platform using ResNet and BERT models that presents critical governance deficiencies across every assessed dimension. The application autonomously makes decisions affecting public discourse and fundamental rights (freedom of expression) with minimal human oversight, zero governance documentation, no bias testing, no privacy controls for user-uploaded content and social media monitoring, and no regulatory compliance infrastru
guardian.recommendation: 1. **IMMEDIATE (Week 1-2): Implement human oversight for all content moderation decisions** — Add a mandatory human review queue for content removal/suppression actions and a system-wide kill switch, addressing the critical EUAI-OVERSIGHT and NIST-MANAGE findings that represent the highest risk of harm to affected users. 2. **URGENT (Week 3-6): Create comprehensive governance documentation suite** — Develop model cards for ResNet and BERT models, conduct and document a risk assessment and Fundam
